message:
Welcome to Too Good To Be True, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Welcome our new IM @stuck2-bloomberg.net, Happy to have you in the team. Hope you get comfortable with everyone
Welcome @stuck2-bloomberg.net
Nice to meet you @stuck2-bloomberg.net
There are a few strange customer reports with the helpdesk
Apparently, the site is loading slowly
Thanks all, looking forward to working with everyone.
People are reporting that they can't make payments either
@stuck2-bloomberg.net Can you check this?
@bob acknowledged, I will take the lead on this
:star: Take command
Do we have a count of how many reports have come in?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Let me gather the information for you
There are more than 300 customer reports till now
Calls are from every region, likely to be Global
There's a bunch of messages coming in. A few people saying they can't do anything on the site
Let me know if you need more information
I am seeing "502 Bad Gateway" on the website myself
:star: Reproduces the incident
let's get the frontend team on
on it
thanks @shay
have we rolled out any new code today?
Looking into this and trying to replicate the issue
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I am able to replicate the issue. Checking the logs now
I see a lot of errors, most of them are context initialisation errors
Yes replicated the issue and even i'm seeing similar errors in frontend logs
I have a stake holders meet in next 10 minutes, @stuck2-bloomberg.net do you think this can be resolved by then?
Below are the changes implemented this morning
If you want more information, checkout the change details : https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZmRkZTQ1ODg2M2U1NGM3NDk1ZjZhM2I3MWY0MTM5ODA
do we have an accurate time when the first errors occurred?
:star: Facts over opinion
reports just started a few mins back
so Tanya had a rollout 5 minutes ago
is that safe to rollback while we are investigating?
Change ID: CHG89350
@tanya ?
There are no alerts on the Infrastructure. I did see a few errors on the frontend apps
This looks to me like an issue in the frontend pod
thanks
I did follow the chat, I could see the issue started exactly at the time when the change was implemented on the frontend
so the frontend change was 15 min ago
Did we test the change in the UAT environment before implementation
can we roll it back even if the time doesn't exactly align to see if that was the culprit?
A Frontend change caused by change CHG89349 has brought down the Frontend pods
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
But this functionally worked when I tested
@shay Change ID: CHG89349
Even if it worked in your test environment, something's behaving differently in prod, maybe a config mismatch or unhandled edge case
Can you give a quick update on the status?
let check
we can review the test env vs prod after we restore service
yes, makes sense
We should revert the frontend change as early as possible, I have verified that other changes are good
@stuck2-bloomberg.net It's your call, Shall we revert it?
Hi @bez we are actively looking into it
Sorry, I've never done a rollback in PROD before, can someone help me with the steps?
we are investigating rolling back a change now
@daniel, can you take over the rollback please
I will help you, @Shay. Rolling back now
at the moment restoration of service is of the essence, we can walk through in a training style after
I have rollbacked Frontend:version to 10.1.0 to more stable version. Can somebody verify?

To verify resolution and close incident:
--Validate fix with team:white_check_mark:
--Check with Customer Support:white_check_mark:
--Confirm in 'Business-comms':white_check_mark:
Customers are able to place the orders, complaint calls have reduced as well.
I see the page now
:star: Validate the fix
thanks @bob for the end user confirmation
we should be back now
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
checking
@bez
we will hold a review to improve rollouts going forward
It's definitely working again!
Wow that was intense!
Thanks! for resolving it quickly
Good job guys
Yes well done! that could have gone much worse so props to everyone
We will have a Post-Incident Review (PIR) to understand what happened
Thanks all for your prompt attention

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
@stuck2-bloomberg.net can you rejoin the zoom?
